column1: solveigo: wait, so why are there four black ones already on the table?
pathogenix: Because you get a handicap
solveigo: wait, so why are there four black ones already on the table?
pathogenix: Because you get a handicap
C[pathogenix: white has to be aggressive in a handicap game since black already controls the board
C[solveigo: sure. do your finest :) i don't mind losing as i need to learn strategies anyway
pathogenix: For the first few games, just focus on keeping your stones connected, and mine disconnected if possible
pathogenix: A lot of go is tactical reading and a feel for shape, and you can only get that by playing
pathogenix: All the strategy can come later
C[pathogenix: Except make a big ugly ball of stones where a nice group used to be
solveigo: you can't put one there anyway
pathogenix: You can undo if you like
pathogenix: But you're right, that move doesn't do anything
C[solveigo: i'll work on my speed in the next game...
pathogenix: Speed is not a requirement :)
solveigo: time not important, only life important
C[solveigo: i know... 5th element. but it's a good quote.
C[pathogenix: This game is not going well for white
C[solveigo: that's you, not me. hurrah
pathogenix: that's your first mistake, though, you're doing very well
pathogenix: That gives me some breathing room, I thought I might have to give up this corner
pathogenix: and then the game would basically be lost for white
C[pathogenix: It would have been better to connect solidly. White can capture at E2
C[pathogenix: good :) Now white has to connect, but black *also* has to connect or white can cut through again
solveigo: i think i was born to go
pathogenix: The game is nearly finished
C[solveigo: well. handicap definitely helps a lot
pathogenix: It's there for both players :) Black gets a helping hand and white gets a bigger challenge
pathogenix: Your opening was very nice, I was gravely concerned
solveigo: that's some fast google translating
pathogenix: I guessed takk because it's the same in Danish and ... Swedish?
C[pathogenix: Black should have blocked at B5, now white can push in - see?
C[pathogenix: Do you know about life and death? The two-eyes thing?
C[solveigo: uhm. i think i saw some two eye thing in the rules but my buffer was a bit full at the time so i didn't read it properly.
C[pathogenix: I'll show you quickly after the game.

pathogenix: There's nothing you can really do on this side of the board. The action is all still at the bottom. There's a white stone and a black stone in atari. Once that's cleaned up, the game is done :)
solveigo: hmh. well i can't really put mine anywhere at the bottom apart from inside circles at bottom let... which seems pointless?
solveigo: if i put them on G1 or H1 you'll just take them.
pathogenix: :) Black can capture a white stone at E3, and then white has to connect at F2 and the game's done
pathogenix: Otherwise white can capture at D1
solveigo: no... that's a suicide move right?
pathogenix: and then black connects at C2
solveigo: i thought it was a suicide setup
pathogenix: It's not suicide if you capture
pathogenix: So does everybody else at first
pathogenix: actually black is still in atari at D3
pathogenix: and should connect... also D1...
pathogenix: But we can skip those if you want
pathogenix: Or you take one and I'll take the other
solveigo: yup. see it now. thought you weren't able to put yours in there as per suicide misunderstanding
pathogenix: It's the kind of move that gets left until the end because it's only worth a single point
pathogenix: But it needs finishing off for the scoring algorithm to work
C[pathogenix: If you pass, I'll connect, and then we both pass and you've completed your first game
pathogenix: You can't immediately capture my stone back because of the Ko rule
solveigo: well i can just put a stone in top left corner... but that won't change anything though
solveigo: so better i just pass then?
pathogenix: Yeah, the upper left is dead, placing a stone there just gives me an extra point
solveigo: oh ok. i didn't read the scoring details fully either...
C[pathogenix: :) I'll explain that in a sec, too
C[pathogenix: Okay, if you accept the removed stones, I'll explain what's going on
pathogenix: But I should probably come over
pathogenix: First time on the 19 x 19?
solveigo: yes. also i'm on the motorway
solveigo: and the connection is a bit waffly
pathogenix: No probs, Corners first, then sides, then centre. On the big board there aren't any points to be made in the centre, comparatively :)
pathogenix: I hope you're not driving
C[pathogenix: No probs, Corners first, then sides, then centre. On the big board there aren't any points to be made in the centre, comparatively :)
pathogenix: I hope you're not driving
